summary: Solid soundboard capture of a mid‑80s show with a standout Stranger → China Cat Sunflower → Rider sequence, but heat‑related venue issues and uneven drum mix give the performance a mixed feel.
review: The Miller‑remastered soundboard delivers clear and well‑balanced audio that lets listeners enjoy the typical fast ’85 Dead vibe. Highlights include a smoking Stranger > China Cat Sunflower > Rider medley, Alabama Getaway opener and a magical Friend of the Devil, while tracks like He's Gone > Truckin' and Day Tripper suffer from sloppy playing and drum problems. Overall the show is solid but hampered by a scorching day and occasional mix glitches.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and soundboard quality, noting several standout songs; a minority highlight serious flaws (drums, heat, venue), leading to an overall rating just below four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, balanced board capture with good clarity; some listeners note drum mix inconsistencies and occasional ambient noise but no major drop‑outs.

standout_songs: Stranger > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical fast 1985 set with a solid opener and strong middle, though the scorching heat and venue logistics made parts feel uneven.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed remarks – some note slower playing while others praise his tone during the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Positive nod to Brent's keyboards; reviewers consider any Jerry‑Brent combo a win.
band_member_comments.Drums: Several complaints about drum mix and looping issues, especially on Day Tripper.
